items.subjective_preview: Patient presents for migraine evaluation. Reports 3-4 migraines per month, down from 6-7 before starting topiramate 50mg daily. Migraines are unilater...
items.objective_preview: Vitals: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 70 bpm, Temp 98.5F. Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No papilledema on fundoscopic exam. ...
items.assessment: Migraine without aura - partially controlled on topiramate, frequency reduced but not at goal of <2/month
items.plan:
1. Continue topiramate 50mg daily - may increase to 75mg if frequency does not improve further
2. Continue sumatriptan 50mg PRN for acute attacks (limit to <10 doses/month)
3. Brain MRI without and with contrast ordered to rule out secondary causes
4. Maintain headache diary
5. Discuss CGRP inhibitor therapy if MRI normal and frequency does not improve
6. Follow-up in 2 months after MRI
